bibliographic_data.title: FilterPlus: A real-time content filtering extension for Google Chrome
bibliographic_data.abstract: Content filtering in web browsers is a tedious process for most of the people, because of several reasons. By blocking JavaScript, Cookies and Popups, end users can ensure maximum protection from browser based attacks and vulnerabilities. In order to accomplish this, we built an extension for Google Chrome which allows users to have easy control over what they wish to receive from a web page. We also build this extension in such a way that it remembers the choice of options made by the user for every URLs, thereby letting users create rules for websites they visit.

core_content.novelty_claim: The FilterPlus extension provides a real-time content filtering mechanism for Google Chrome that allows users to control what they receive from web pages, including blocking JavaScript, Cookies, Popups, and Notifications.
core_content.key_methodology: The extension is built using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, with a manifest.json file specifying its permissions and actions. It includes features such as cookie management, image control, popup blocking, and JavaScript notifications. The extension remembers user preferences for each URL visited, allowing users to create rules that are automatically applied.
outcomes: Users can easily control what they receive from web pages.
The extension remembers user preferences for each URL, enabling automatic rule application on revisit.
core_content.full_text_markdown:
# Introduction
Extensions are programs written to enhance the functionality of web browsers. They provide developers a platform to build browser-based applications and help users improve their web browsing experience.

In Google Chrome, extensions are quite popular among installed ones, with many focusing on popup blockers and JavaScript blockers. However, most lack essential features that allow users to control what they see from a webpage. 

Chrome being the most popular web browser has added several security features in recent years, but it still poses risks due to web-based attacks.

This project aims to develop a security extension called FilterPlus which allows users to choose what they want to receive from URLs.

# Basics of Chrome Extensions
Google Chrome extensions are built using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. The essential part is the manifest.json file, which specifies the extension's properties, permissions, and actions. 

The first line of the manifest file specifies the manifest version, with developers currently recommended to specify 'manifest version':2. It includes parameters such as name, description, version, and permissions.

# Design Considerations
Extensions are platform-dependent, meaning an extension written for Chrome will not work on other browsers unless rewritten. Chrome incorporates security features like Safe Browsing, Sandboxing, and Auto-updates to protect against malicious web content.

# Design Principles
The FilterPlus extension was designed following four principles: providing branding, ensuring effectiveness for naive users, minimizing user effort, and making sure that security mechanisms are usable.
